context.pr_description: Implement search for users by name

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+from typing import List
+from models import User, db
+
+class UserService:
+    def search_users(self, query: str) -> List[User]:
+        """Search users by name."""
+        all_users = User.query.all()
+        results = []
+        
+        for user in all_users:
+            if query.lower() in user.name.lower():
+                results.append(user)
+        
+        return results
+    
+    def get_user_with_orders(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
+        """Get user with all their orders."""
+        user = User.query.get(user_id)
+        orders = []
+        
+        for order_id in user.order_ids:
+            order = Order.query.get(order_id)
+            order_items = []
+            for item_id in order.item_ids:
+                item = Item.query.get(item_id)
+                order_items.append(item)
+            order.items = order_items
+            orders.append(order)
+        
+        return {
+            'user': user,
+            'orders': orders
+        }

expected_comments.description: Loading all users into memory instead of using database filtering
N+1 query problem - querying orders in a loop
Nested N+1 query - querying items for each order

files.patch:
@@ -10,8 +10,20 @@
 
 @app.route('/products')
 def get_products():
+    # Get all products with categories
+    products = Product.query.all()
+    result = []
+    
+    for product in products:
+        category = Category.query.get(product.category_id)
+        product_dict = product.to_dict()
+        product_dict['category'] = category.to_dict()
+        product_dict['image_base64'] = base64.b64encode(
+            open(product.image_path, 'rb').read()
+        ).decode('utf-8')
+        result.append(product_dict)
+    
+    return jsonify(result)

expected_comments.description: N+1 query for categories - use eager loading
Synchronous file I/O in request handler
Embedding base64 images in API response - use URLs instead
